statement: This record remains negative evidence on build 26100: the repo did not produce enough supporting proof to promote it into a normal actionable surface.
gating_reason: This record is cross-layer verified and also aligned with a shipped one-click surface.

links.title: Microsoft Support: March 2016 anti-malware platform update for Endpoint Protection clients
links.summary: Microsoft Support publishes ThreatFileHashLogging = 1 under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows Defender as the Windows Defender Antivirus registry setting for SHA1 threat-file hash logging and says event 1120 is recorded when a threat file is detected and hash logging is enabled.

links.title: Microsoft Learn: Create indicators for files
links.summary: Microsoft says file-hash computation is enabled by the Defender Antivirus MpEngine policy 'Enable File Hash Computation' and notes that file indicators support PE files only.

links.title: Microsoft Support: March 2016 anti-malware platform update for Endpoint Protection clients
links.summary: Microsoft documents event 1120 as the file-hash-logging event and says it is logged only when ThreatFileHashLogging is enabled.

links.title: Microsoft Learn: Demonstrate cloud-delivered protection
links.summary: Microsoft publishes a password-protected BaFS Sample PE file for Defender cloud-protection demonstrations. The sample used in the VM pass extracted to a 123904-byte PE with SHA256 670b00e90a7c9eb7ac6674441551e7764a8364c26e44dcc92474a9abcfac4c04.

links.title: Local Defender dumps and traces for ThreatFileHashLogging and EnableFileHashComputation
links.summary: The local dump mirrors show ThreatFileHashLogging on the Defender root path, EnableFileHashComputation on the Policy Manager alias, and the 25H2 trace mirror also shows the non-policy MpEngine surface.

links.title: Original high-risk snapshot had Defender disabled
links.summary: The original high-risk snapshot had WinDefend and WdNisSvc set to Disabled and Get-MpComputerStatus reported 'Not running', which would have made Defender policy validation misleading until the VM was repaired.
